name: ChatGPT
privacy_label: you can turn off chat history & training
why_this_one: They are awful

name: Claude
privacy_label: doesn't train on your chats by default
why_this_one: Especially good at longer writing careful reasoning and working through big documents in one go.

name: Google Gemini
privacy_label: review activity settings to limit data use
price_label: free tier; Advanced via Google One
why_this_one: Handy if you live in Gmail Docs and Android. Huge context window for very long inputs.

name: Microsoft Copilot
privacy_label: enterprise data protections on work accounts
why_this_one: Convenient if you already use Windows and Microsoft 365 — it sits right inside the apps you have.

name: Perplexity
privacy_label: can opt out of data used for training
ease_label: works like a smarter search engine
why_this_one: Best when you want answers with sources cited — it searches the live web and shows where facts came from.

name: GitHub Copilot
privacy_label: code suggestions not retained on paid tiers
why_this_one: The most established coding helper — autocompletes code right inside VS Code and other editors.

name: Cursor
privacy_label: privacy mode keeps code off their servers
ease_label: a full code editor — some setup
why_this_one: An AI-first code editor that understands your whole project. A favourite of serious developers.

name: Whisper
privacy_label: runs on your own machine — nothing leaves it
ease_label: a bit techy: needs the command line
why_this_one: Open-source transcription you can run entirely offline. Nothing is uploaded — ideal for sensitive interviews.

name: MacWhisper
privacy_label: transcribes locally on your Mac
price_label: free tier; Pro is a one-off ~$60
ease_label: friendly app — drag in a file
why_this_one: Whisper's accuracy wrapped in a simple Mac app. Local transcription without touching the command line.

name: Otter.ai
privacy_label: stores your recordings on their servers
why_this_one: Great for live meeting notes and speaker labels. Note it keeps your recordings in the cloud.

name: Llama
privacy_label: open weights — run it fully offline
ease_label: needs a runner like Ollama to use
why_this_one: Meta's open model family. The backbone of most local-AI setups when you want full control and privacy.

name: Mistral Le Chat
privacy_label: EU-based with opt-out of training
why_this_one: A fast capable European option. Many of its models are also open-weight if you want to self-host later.

name: DeepSeek
privacy_label: data stored on servers in China
why_this_one: Strong reasoning and coding at a remarkably low price. Check the data policy before sensitive use.

name: Ollama
privacy_label: everything runs and stays on your computer
ease_label: a bit techy to set up
why_this_one: The easiest way to download and run open models locally. Free private and offline once it's set up.

name: LM Studio
privacy_label: models run locally — nothing uploaded
ease_label: friendly app for running local models
why_this_one: A point-and-click app for running open models on your own machine — local AI without the terminal.

name: NotebookLM
privacy_label: says it doesn't train on your uploads
ease_label: upload your docs and ask away
why_this_one: Point it at your own documents and it answers only from them with citations. Excellent for research and study.

name: DeepL
privacy_label: Pro deletes text after translating
ease_label: paste text and pick a language
why_this_one: Widely considered the most natural-sounding translator — noticeably better than the free generic ones.

name: Grammarly
privacy_label: processes your text on their servers
why_this_one: Catches grammar and tone issues as you write across email docs and the web. The gentle everyday writing helper.

name: Poe
privacy_label: depends on the underlying model you pick
why_this_one: Lets you try many different AI models in one place — useful when you're not sure which one suits you.

name: DeepGram
privacy_label: can request no data retention on paid plans
why_this_one: Fast accurate transcription built for developers. Reach for this when you're wiring transcription into an app.

name: Cohere Command
privacy_label: business-focused with opt-out controls
why_this_one: A business-oriented text model with strong search and retrieval features. Aimed at developers and teams.

name: Jasper
privacy_label: stores content on their platform
why_this_one: Built for marketing teams: on-brand campaigns blog posts and ads with templates. Paid only, no free tier.

name: xAI Grok
privacy_label: review xAI data controls before sensitive use
why_this_one: xAI flagship models available through OpenRouter, useful for comparing another major general-purpose assistant family.

name: Qwen
privacy_label: review provider policy before sensitive use
why_this_one: A strong open-model family with high-context general and coding models available through OpenRouter.

name: Amazon Nova
privacy_label: AWS-hosted model family; review Bedrock data controls
why_this_one: Amazon's Nova models add a major cloud-provider family to the benchmark set.

name: Moonshot Kimi
privacy_label: review provider policy before sensitive use
why_this_one: Kimi is a strong long-context and coding-focused model family available through OpenRouter.

name: Z.ai GLM
privacy_label: review provider policy before sensitive use
why_this_one: GLM models are high-context general assistants worth comparing against the major US and open model families.

name: MiniMax
privacy_label: review provider policy before sensitive use
why_this_one: MiniMax offers inexpensive high-context models that are useful for value and long-context comparisons.

name: AI21 Jamba
privacy_label: enterprise-focused API; review provider data controls
why_this_one: AI21's Jamba model adds another enterprise-focused long-context model family to the benchmark set.
